verses.text: The Spirit sets him in a valley of 'very dry' bones; God asks, 'can these bones live?' He answers, 'O Lord... thou knowest.' Hopeless to sight. What seems utterly dead is not beyond God's power.
The Spirit sets him in a valley of 'very dry' bones; God asks, 'can these bones live?' He answers, 'O Lord... thou knowest.' Hopeless to sight. What seems utterly dead is not beyond God's power.
The Spirit sets him in a valley of 'very dry' bones; God asks, 'can these bones live?' He answers, 'O Lord... thou knowest.' Hopeless to sight. What seems utterly dead is not beyond God's power.
He must prophesy to the bones: God will lay sinew and flesh on them, give them breath, 'and ye shall live.' The word of life. God's promise works where no hope remains.
He must prophesy to the bones: God will lay sinew and flesh on them, give them breath, 'and ye shall live.' The word of life. God's promise works where no hope remains.
He must prophesy to the bones: God will lay sinew and flesh on them, give them breath, 'and ye shall live.' The word of life. God's promise works where no hope remains.
As he prophesies, a noise and shaking, and bone joins bone with flesh, 'but there was no breath.' Form without life. Outward order is not yet living power.
As he prophesies, a noise and shaking, and bone joins bone with flesh, 'but there was no breath.' Form without life. Outward order is not yet living power.
He prophesies to the wind; the breath enters, and they live and stand, 'an exceeding great army.' Life given. Only the breath of God turns the form into a living host.
He prophesies to the wind; the breath enters, and they live and stand, 'an exceeding great army.' Life given. Only the breath of God turns the form into a living host.
The bones are Israel, who say, 'Our hope is lost'; God will open their graves and bring them out. Despair answered. God revives a people who had given themselves up for dead.
The bones are Israel, who say, 'Our hope is lost'; God will open their graves and bring them out. Despair answered. God revives a people who had given themselves up for dead.
They shall know the LORD when He opens their graves and puts His Spirit in them. The Spirit's work. Resurrection life is the proof that God is the LORD.
They shall know the LORD when He opens their graves and puts His Spirit in them. The Spirit's work. Resurrection life is the proof that God is the LORD.
He takes two sticks, one for Judah and one for Joseph, and joins them as one. A sign of union. God will heal the long division of His people.
He takes two sticks, one for Judah and one for Joseph, and joins them as one. A sign of union. God will heal the long division of His people.
He takes two sticks, one for Judah and one for Joseph, and joins them as one. A sign of union. God will heal the long division of His people.
When asked the meaning, he says the two nations shall be one in God's hand. Reunion promised. The Shepherd gathers His scattered flock into one.
When asked the meaning, he says the two nations shall be one in God's hand. Reunion promised. The Shepherd gathers His scattered flock into one.
When asked the meaning, he says the two nations shall be one in God's hand. Reunion promised. The Shepherd gathers His scattered flock into one.
He will gather Israel, make them one nation under one king, and cleanse them, 'and they shall be my people.' Renewed covenant. Gathering and cleansing go together.
He will gather Israel, make them one nation under one king, and cleanse them, 'and they shall be my people.' Renewed covenant. Gathering and cleansing go together.
He will gather Israel, make them one nation under one king, and cleanse them, 'and they shall be my people.' Renewed covenant. Gathering and cleansing go together.
'David my servant shall be king over them,' and they shall walk in God's judgments and dwell in the land for ever. The Messiah reigns. Christ, David's Son, rules His united people.
'David my servant shall be king over them,' and they shall walk in God's judgments and dwell in the land for ever. The Messiah reigns. Christ, David's Son, rules His united people.
He will make 'an everlasting covenant of peace' and set His sanctuary among them for ever. God dwells with them. The crown of all His promises is His abiding presence.
He will make 'an everlasting covenant of peace' and set His sanctuary among them for ever. God dwells with them. The crown of all His promises is His abiding presence.
